source_paper_title: Denoising Diffusion Probabilistic Models Are Optimally Adaptive to Unknown Low Dimensionality
context:
Page 16, Section 6 (Discussion)

To begin with, it remains unclear how the DDPM sampler adapts to unknown low dimensionality when other metrics (e.g., the total-variation distance or the Wasserstein distance) are used to measure distributional discrepancy.
title: Iteration complexity of DDPM adaptive to intrinsic dimension under total variation or Wasserstein discrepancy
latex:
Given integers \(d\ge 1\) and \(k\ge 1\), let \(p_{\mathrm{data}}\) be a probability distribution on \(\mathbb{R}^d\) with support \(\mathcal{X}_{\mathrm{data}}\subset \mathbb{R}^d\) satisfying the metric-entropy intrinsic-dimension condition: there exist universal constants \(C_{\mathrm{cover}}>0\) and \(C_0>0\) such that for \(\varepsilon_0:=k^{-C_0}\),
\[
\log N_{\mathrm{cover}}(\mathcal{X}_{\mathrm{data}},\|\cdot\|_2,\varepsilon_0)\le C_{\mathrm{cover}}\,k\log(1/\varepsilon_0).
\]
Consider the Ornstein--Uhlenbeck forward diffusion \((X_t)_{t\in[0,T]}\) defined by
\[
X_t\stackrel{d}{=}\sqrt{1-\sigma_t^2}\,X_0+\sigma_t W,\quad \sigma_t:=\sqrt{1-e^{-2t}},\quad X_0\sim p_{\mathrm{data}},\; W\sim\mathcal{N}(0,I_d),
\]
with \(X_0\) independent of \(W\). Let \(q_t\) denote the law of \(X_t\).

Fix \(\delta\in(0,1)\), an accuracy level \(\varepsilon\in(0,1)\), and suppose the true score functions \(s_t(x):=\nabla \log q_t(x)\) are available. Let \(p_{\mathrm{out}}\) be the output distribution of the discrete-time DDPM sampler with some time grid \(0=t_0<t_1<\cdots<t_N=T\) and the standard DDPM update rule (as in Ho et al. (2020), equivalently equation (2) in the paper) using the true scores.

Problem: Determine whether there exist universal constants \(c,C>0\) and a choice of time horizon \(T\) and discretization schedule such that for every such \(p_{\mathrm{data}}\) and every \(\varepsilon\in(0,1)\), one can guarantee with
\[
N \le C\,\frac{k\,\mathrm{polylog}(k/\varepsilon)}{\varepsilon^2}
\]
that the DDPM output satisfies an intrinsic-dimension-adaptive convergence bound under a metric \(\mathsf{D}\) stronger than KL, for example:
- total variation: \(\mathsf{D}(q_\delta,p_{\mathrm{out}})=\|q_\delta-p_{\mathrm{out}}\|_{\mathrm{TV}}\le \varepsilon\), or
- Wasserstein: \(\mathsf{D}(q_\delta,p_{\mathrm{out}})=W_2(q_\delta,p_{\mathrm{out}})\le \varepsilon\).

If such a bound is impossible in this generality, characterize the weakest additional assumptions (e.g., smoothness of the noisy score, curvature/regularity of \(\mathcal{X}_{\mathrm{data}}\), log-concavity, etc.) under which an \(N\) scaling nearly linear in \(k\) can be achieved for \(\|\cdot\|_{\mathrm{TV}}\) or \(W_2\).
open_problem_quote: To begin with, it remains unclear how the DDPM sampler adapts to unknown low dimensionality when other metrics (e.g., the total-variation distance or the Wasserstein distance) are used to measure distributional discrepancy.
subject_classification: Stochastic Processes and Generative Modeling (Diffusion Models)
